verses.text: A centurion's servant is sick; the elders plead, for he loves their nation and built a synagogue. A worthy Gentile. His kindness commends him to the Jews.
A centurion's servant is sick; the elders plead, for he loves their nation and built a synagogue. A worthy Gentile. His kindness commends him to the Jews.
A centurion's servant is sick; the elders plead, for he loves their nation and built a synagogue. A worthy Gentile. His kindness commends him to the Jews.
A centurion's servant is sick; the elders plead, for he loves their nation and built a synagogue. A worthy Gentile. His kindness commends him to the Jews.
A centurion's servant is sick; the elders plead, for he loves their nation and built a synagogue. A worthy Gentile. His kindness commends him to the Jews.
'Lord... I am not worthy... speak the word only'; I too am a man under authority. Humble faith. He trusts Christ's bare word to heal at a distance.
'Lord... I am not worthy... speak the word only'; I too am a man under authority. Humble faith. He trusts Christ's bare word to heal at a distance.
'Lord... I am not worthy... speak the word only'; I too am a man under authority. Humble faith. He trusts Christ's bare word to heal at a distance.
Jesus marvels, 'I have not found so great faith... in Israel'; the servant is healed. Faith honoured. The outsider's trust shames the privileged.
Jesus marvels, 'I have not found so great faith... in Israel'; the servant is healed. Faith honoured. The outsider's trust shames the privileged.
At Nain He meets a widow's dead son; moved with compassion, 'Weep not'; 'Young man... arise'; he sits up. Tender power. He raises the dead and dries a mother's tears.
At Nain He meets a widow's dead son; moved with compassion, 'Weep not'; 'Young man... arise'; he sits up. Tender power. He raises the dead and dries a mother's tears.
At Nain He meets a widow's dead son; moved with compassion, 'Weep not'; 'Young man... arise'; he sits up. Tender power. He raises the dead and dries a mother's tears.
At Nain He meets a widow's dead son; moved with compassion, 'Weep not'; 'Young man... arise'; he sits up. Tender power. He raises the dead and dries a mother's tears.
At Nain He meets a widow's dead son; moved with compassion, 'Weep not'; 'Young man... arise'; he sits up. Tender power. He raises the dead and dries a mother's tears.
Fear comes on all; 'a great prophet is risen,' God hath visited His people; the news spreads. Wondering awe. His mercy proclaims that God has drawn near.
Fear comes on all; 'a great prophet is risen,' God hath visited His people; the news spreads. Wondering awe. His mercy proclaims that God has drawn near.
John's disciples ask, 'Art thou he that should come?' A faith tested. Even John, in prison, has his hour of doubt.
John's disciples ask, 'Art thou he that should come?' A faith tested. Even John, in prison, has his hour of doubt.
John's disciples ask, 'Art thou he that should come?' A faith tested. Even John, in prison, has his hour of doubt.
Tell John the blind see, the dead rise, 'the poor... have the gospel'; blessed is he not offended. Works speak. The deeds of mercy prove who He is.
Tell John the blind see, the dead rise, 'the poor... have the gospel'; blessed is he not offended. Works speak. The deeds of mercy prove who He is.
Tell John the blind see, the dead rise, 'the poor... have the gospel'; blessed is he not offended. Works speak. The deeds of mercy prove who He is.
Of John, more than a prophet; yet 'the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.' Privilege of grace. To be in Christ's kingdom is higher than the highest before it.
Of John, more than a prophet; yet 'the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.' Privilege of grace. To be in Christ's kingdom is higher than the highest before it.
Of John, more than a prophet; yet 'the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.' Privilege of grace. To be in Christ's kingdom is higher than the highest before it.
Of John, more than a prophet; yet 'the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.' Privilege of grace. To be in Christ's kingdom is higher than the highest before it.
Of John, more than a prophet; yet 'the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.' Privilege of grace. To be in Christ's kingdom is higher than the highest before it.
The people justify God, but the Pharisees 'rejected the counsel of God against themselves.' Self-harm. To refuse God's call is to wrong one's own soul.
The people justify God, but the Pharisees 'rejected the counsel of God against themselves.' Self-harm. To refuse God's call is to wrong one's own soul.
This generation is like sulky children, rejecting John and the Son of man; 'wisdom is justified of... her children.' Never pleased. The fault-finder objects to all God sends.
This generation is like sulky children, rejecting John and the Son of man; 'wisdom is justified of... her children.' Never pleased. The fault-finder objects to all God sends.
This generation is like sulky children, rejecting John and the Son of man; 'wisdom is justified of... her children.' Never pleased. The fault-finder objects to all God sends.
This generation is like sulky children, rejecting John and the Son of man; 'wisdom is justified of... her children.' Never pleased. The fault-finder objects to all God sends.
This generation is like sulky children, rejecting John and the Son of man; 'wisdom is justified of... her children.' Never pleased. The fault-finder objects to all God sends.
A sinful woman weeps at His feet, washes them with tears, and anoints them. Lavish love. Her gratitude pours itself out before Christ.
A sinful woman weeps at His feet, washes them with tears, and anoints them. Lavish love. Her gratitude pours itself out before Christ.
A sinful woman weeps at His feet, washes them with tears, and anoints them. Lavish love. Her gratitude pours itself out before Christ.
The Pharisee judges her; in the parable of two debtors, he loves most who is forgiven most. Forgiven, loving. Great pardon awakens great love.
The Pharisee judges her; in the parable of two debtors, he loves most who is forgiven most. Forgiven, loving. Great pardon awakens great love.
The Pharisee judges her; in the parable of two debtors, he loves most who is forgiven most. Forgiven, loving. Great pardon awakens great love.
The Pharisee judges her; in the parable of two debtors, he loves most who is forgiven most. Forgiven, loving. Great pardon awakens great love.
The Pharisee judges her; in the parable of two debtors, he loves most who is forgiven most. Forgiven, loving. Great pardon awakens great love.
Her many sins are forgiven, 'for she loved much'; to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little. Love's measure. The depth of love shows the sense of pardon.
Her many sins are forgiven, 'for she loved much'; to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little. Love's measure. The depth of love shows the sense of pardon.
Her many sins are forgiven, 'for she loved much'; to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little. Love's measure. The depth of love shows the sense of pardon.
Her many sins are forgiven, 'for she loved much'; to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little. Love's measure. The depth of love shows the sense of pardon.
'Thy sins are forgiven... thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace.' Faith saves. Not her tears but her faith brought her pardon and peace.
'Thy sins are forgiven... thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace.' Faith saves. Not her tears but her faith brought her pardon and peace.
